summary: Start M6-START-TO-VICTORY-SIM-LOOP-001: implement deterministic start-to-victory Alpha simulation loop through authoritative sim-core, sim-ai, GameCommand, scheduler, event, query, save, replay, Node runner, and Worker-compatible paths.
acceptance_criteria: At least one accepted M6 Alpha scenario can run from start state to one victory outcome entirely through authoritative sim-core state, scheduler, GameCommand, event, query, save, and replay paths.
Victory, defeat, and continued-play terminal states are explicit, deterministic, versioned, and hash-covered in Node runner and Worker-compatible contract tests.
The loop integrates M2 economy/routes, M3 governance/succession, M4 campaign/postwar systems, M5 playable loop evidence, and M6 diplomacy/legitimacy/policy/event systems without hidden UI mutation.
Manual node battle, complex naval warfare, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code scripts, object-graph saves, and all-purpose manager abstractions remain excluded.
required_tests: Start-to-victory scenario tests cover deterministic terminal state, save/load mid-run, malformed command rejection, no P0/P1 data corruption, and Node/Worker hash parity.
constraints: Only edit allowed_paths. Do not edit UI/client/map/content-schema/content-runtime/content-source/tools/CI/docs/status/manifests/validation reports.
Run the loop through authoritative sim-core state, scheduler, GameCommand, event, query, save, and replay paths. No hidden UI mutation or out-of-band rescue hooks.
Use the same command paths for player and AI; no special AI-only state mutation.
Terminal victory, defeat, and continued-play states must be explicit, deterministic, versioned, and hash-covered.
Integrate accepted M2 economy/routes, M3 governance/succession, M4 campaign/postwar, M5 playable-loop evidence, and M6 diplomacy/legitimacy/policy-event substrates at minimal Alpha scope.
Do not implement manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code scripts, object-graph saves, M7 full content, or M8 release stability.
Same seed, initial state, and command sequence must produce same state hash in Node and Worker-compatible paths.
requested_action: Implement in this branch, run required/focused tests as feasible, write a structured REVIEW handoff to project/messages/outbox, and route back to systems_architect via lead. Do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED or merge.
